content: src/warren-config/ module mirrors src/projects/ + src/registry/ file layout: errors.ts (WarrenConfigUnavailableError + per-file error type), config.ts (static layout constants — no env vars, .warren/ path is hardcoded relative to project clone), schema.ts (zod schemas + parseTriggersConfig/parseDefaultsConfig returning discriminated ParseResult), load.ts (loadWarrenConfig with injected exists/readFile), index.ts (public re-exports).

title: loadWarrenConfig uses missing-vs-malformed envelope, never throws on per-file errors
rationale: Acceptance criteria #2 of pl-5d74 requires the loader to return null entries for missing files (no error) and validation-error entries for malformed files (no throw). Modeled after src/registry/refresh.ts's per-agent skip-don't-throw pattern: a single malformed sibling never hides a healthy one from the operator. Returned shape: { triggers: T|null, defaults: D|null, errors: WarrenConfigFileError[] }. The HTTP, doctor, and UI layers all render the errors envelope verbatim. Only unrecoverable failures (project clone vanished entirely) throw WarrenConfigUnavailableError — same class shape as ProjectUnavailableError so the HTTP layer can map both to a 503 with the same recovery hint.

content: TriggerSchema is a discriminated union on 'kind' — only 'cron' is implemented today, but the discriminator + .strict() shape is locked in now (per pl-5d74 risk #1) so future webhook/manual triggers added by R-06+ slot in as another 'kind:' without a breaking schema rev. .strict() on every object schema makes typos (e.g. 'defaultRoll') fail loudly instead of silently dropping the field.

Cron expression validation is intentionally loose (5 or 6 whitespace-separated tokens, non-empty) — full grammar is R-06 scheduler's job. Duplicating croner's grammar at the schema layer would lock the upstream version and force a schema rev every time croner ships a syntax extension.
